broker_fee_detail: No-fee under FARE Act; listed by Leonidas Realty
broker_message: Hi Christina — I'm very interested in 179 Gelston Ave #4 at $1,649. Could you confirm it's rent-stabilized and share the DHCR rent history / legal rent, and confirm heat and hot water are included? I'd also like total move-in cost and confirmation there's no broker fee. If those check out I'd like to move quickly. Thanks!
broker_questions: Is it genuinely rent-stabilized? Can I see DHCR rent history and legal rent?
Source of the '0% increases 2-3 yrs' claim — a written rider?
How many flights up, any elevator?
Why didn't it rent at $1,785 this spring?
How reliable is winter heat (Class C on record)?
Total move-in costs and any broker fee?
building_risk_rationale: Total HPD (~22) runs above the Fort Hamilton avg (~13) and includes at least one Class C 'no heat' (Apt 3B, open 11/2024) plus a building-wide heat complaint as recent as Feb 2026 — a recurring winter pattern, with prior roach (Class C) and mold/leak filings. Offsetting: zero ECB, zero evictions, zero bedbugs, below-avg 311 (9) and DOB (1); facade/scaffold permits signed off. Exact open count inconsistent, unverified. Net: manageable Medium.
commute_notes: Far end of the R at the 95th St terminal, ~1 block — always a seat but the longest ride in the borough, ~55-70 min to Midtown on the R local. No express nearby.
concessions_to_ask: get rent-stabilized status and legal rent in writing
confirm heat/hot water included in writing
confirm no broker fee and move-in cost
cons: 4th-floor walk-up with only 2 photos and no floor plan/sqft
Recurring heat/hot-water pattern incl. Class C 'no heat'
Longest commute in the borough — R local only, ~55-70 min to Midtown
leverage_notes: Already cheapest studio in the comp set and below fair range; just cut $136. Little room to push rent — the smarter play is locking the stabilization/frozen-rent claim in writing.
noise_risks: quiet residential block; low ambient noise
plain_english: Best-value pick on paper: a no-fee, heat-included, rent-stabilized studio at $1,649 — cheapest comparable studio around, freshly cut from $1,785. The prize is stabilization with flat renewals, so get that in writing on tour (DHCR + legal rent). Trade-offs: 4th-floor walk-up, thin photos, longest-in-borough R-only commute, and a recurring winter heat problem. If the stairs work for you and stabilization checks out, this is close to a strong apply.
pros: Cheapest studio in the comp set ($1,649), just cut from $1,785, heat/hot water included, no fee
Rent-stabilized claim with flat renewals — strong long-term value if verified
Quiet block, live-in super, R terminal one block away (guaranteed seat)

red_flags.how_to_verify: Climb it on tour; check stair condition.
red_flags.why: Four flights, no elevator — a real daily burden.

red_flags.issue: Only 2 photos, no floor plan or sqft

red_flags.how_to_verify: Request DHCR rent history; get legal rent in writing.
Ask super; check HPD Online; talk to tenants.
red_flags.issue: Building heat/hot-water history incl. Class C 'no heat' and Feb-2026 complaint
red_flags.why: Heating reliability matters on a top floor.
